title: Deal enumerations
description: Enumerations for deal management, participant roles, and asset classification

DealStage.description: Stage in the deal lifecycle, representing the current phase of work. SOURCING: Deal has been identified but not yet evaluated. SCREENING: Initial assessment of fit with fund strategy and return profile. DUE_DILIGENCE: Detailed analysis of financials, legal, operations, and market. IC_REVIEW: Investment Committee review and approval process (IC Memo stage). TERM_SHEET: Negotiating and executing indicative terms with counterparty. DOCUMENTATION: Drafting and negotiating definitive legal agreements. CLOSING: Executing final documents, funding, and conditions precedent. ACTIVE: Deal has closed and the investment is live. MONITORING: Ongoing portfolio monitoring phase (post-close). EXIT: Actively pursuing exit (sale, IPO, refinancing, or wind-down). CLOSED: Deal has been fully exited and realized. PASSED: Deal was evaluated but declined — no investment made. Example: 'DUE_DILIGENCE'

DealRole.description: Role a participant plays in a deal. LEAD: Primary sponsor or investor driving the deal — controls terms, governance, and board seats. CO_LEAD: Co-leads alongside the lead, typically with significant allocation and shared governance rights. CO_INVESTOR: Invests alongside lead/co-lead, usually with smaller allocation and limited governance. SYNDICATE_MEMBER: Participates in a syndicated credit facility or club deal. ADVISOR: Provides advisory services (financial, strategic, or operational) but does not invest. ARRANGER: Structures and arranges the financing (common in credit deals — the bank that syndicates the loan). AGENT: Administrative or collateral agent for a credit facility, responsible for servicing and lender communication. Example: 'LEAD'

AssetClass.description: Broad asset class classification for a deal asset. Assigned at the DealAsset level, not the Deal level, because a single deal can comprise components across multiple asset classes (e.g., an LBO with private credit facilities and an equity co-invest). PRIVATE_EQUITY: Direct equity investment in a private company (buyout, growth equity). VENTURE_CAPITAL: Equity investment in early- or growth-stage companies. PRIVATE_CREDIT: Privately negotiated debt — term loans, direct lending, mezzanine, unitranche. PUBLIC_CREDIT: Broadly syndicated or traded debt — leveraged loans, high-yield bonds, CLOs. REAL_ESTATE: Direct property investment or real estate debt. INFRASTRUCTURE: Investments in infrastructure assets (energy, transport, digital). NATURAL_RESOURCES: Investments in natural resource assets (mining, timber, agriculture, energy). SECONDARIES: Purchase of existing LP interests or fund portfolios on the secondary market. Example: 'PRIVATE_CREDIT'

IssuerType.description: Classification of the entity on the other side of a deal — the issuer, borrower, or target. CORPORATE: A private or public operating company (e.g., LBO target, direct lending borrower). SOVEREIGN: A national government or sovereign entity (e.g., sovereign debt, government-backed infrastructure). MUNICIPALITY: A sub-national government body — state, city, county, or public authority (e.g., municipal bonds, public infrastructure). SPV: A special purpose vehicle or special purpose entity created for a specific transaction or asset isolation (e.g., securitization vehicle, project finance SPV). FUND: An investment fund being acquired or invested in (e.g., secondary purchase of LP interests, fund-of-funds investment). JOINT_VENTURE: A jointly owned entity formed by two or more parties for a specific project or investment. OTHER: Any issuer type not covered above. Example: 'CORPORATE'

DealAssetType.description: Discriminator indicating which domain entity a DealAsset references. Determines which UUID reference field is populated. FACILITY: References a credit/Facility — populate facility_id. INVESTMENT: References a fund/Investment (portfolio company equity stake) — populate investment_id. PROPERTY: References a real_estate/Property — populate property_id. LP_INTEREST: References a secondaries/LPInterest — populate lp_interest_id. Example: 'FACILITY'
